LinkMyPet.collarInfo.Temperature !== 'normal' ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.PetName !== 'My Pet' ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.PhotoUrl !== 'https://example.com/my-pet.jpg'
```

This filter code checks the following conditions:

1. The date of the collar information creation is not the same as the current date.
2. The battery percentage is less than 100.
3. The temperature reading is not "normal".
4. The pet name is not "My Pet".
5. The photo URL is not "https://example.com/my-pet.jpg".

If any of these conditions are true, the action behavior will be executed.

```
LinkMyPet.petActivity.CreatedAt.hour() % 10 === 0 &&
LinkMyPet.petActivity.YesterdayActivity !== 'active' &&
LinkMyPet.petActivity.TodayActivity !== 'active' &&
LinkMyPet.petActivity.TodayGoalStatus !== 'active'
```

This filter code uses the `hour()` method of the `Meta.currentUserTime` accessor to check if the current hour is a multiple of 10. It then checks if the pet's activity yesterday, today, and today's progress towards the goal are all not active. If all of these conditions are true, the filter code will return `true`, indicating that the action should be executed.

```
const filter = {
 trigger: {
   Twitter.newTweetByUser: {
     Text: {
       $regex: /^(?!.*(RT|FWD))/i,
     },
     UserName: {
       $eq: '[Your Twitter Username]',
     },
     LinkToTweet: {
       $ne: '',
     },
     TweetEmbedCode: {
       $ne: '',
     },
     CreatedAt: {
       $gte: Meta.currentUserTime.hour(8).minute(0).format('YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss'),
       $lte: Meta.currentUserTime.hour(22).minute(0).format('YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss'),
     },
   },
 },
 action: {
   IfNotifications.sendNotification: {
     setMessage: 'New tweet from [Your Twitter Username]',
     setHighPriority: true,
   },
   Feedly.createNewEntryFeedly: {
     setUrl: '[Your Feedly URL]',
   },
 },
 skip: {
   IfNotifications.sendNotification: {
     $or: [
       {
         CreatedAt: {

LinkMyPet.collarInfo.Temperature > 30 &&
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.hour() === Meta.currentUserTime.hour() &&
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.minute() % 30 === 0 &&

```
This filter code checks the temperature reading of the pet's collar, the time the reading was created, the battery level of the collar, the name of the pet, and the photo URL of the pet. If the temperature reading is greater than 30 degrees Celsius, the collar was created within the last hour, the current minute is a multiple of 30, the battery level is not low, the pet has a name, and the pet has a photo URL, then the SMS alert will be sent.

```
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.hour() !== Meta.currentUserTime.hour() ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.minute() !== Meta.currentUserTime.minute() ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.week() !== Meta.currentUserTime.week() ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.isoWeekday() !== Meta.currentUserTime.isoWeekday() ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.CreatedAt.dayOfYear() !== Meta.currentUserTime.dayOfYear() ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.Battery !== "100%" ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.Temperature !== "100%" ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.PetName !== "My Pet" ||
LinkMyPet.collarInfo.PhotoUrl !== "https://example.com/my-pet.jpg"
```
